global.title: Destructive tornado outbreak across the Midwest - NBC News
global.summary: At least 13 tornadoes were reported overnight across five U.S. states, leaving multiple paths of destruction.
global.whyItMatters: Severe weather offers a timely pastoral lens for prayer, compassion, and preaching on fragility, neighbor-love, and practical care.

global.title: Catastrophic floods trigger water rescues - NBC News
global.summary: Flooding in Mississippi and Louisiana prompted boat rescues as rising water put communities at risk.
global.whyItMatters: Disaster response stories help pastors lead congregations in intercession and remind people of the church’s calling to serve in crisis.

global.title: What a great week for peace - TradingView
global.summary: Reuters highlights a week shaped by peace developments, set against wider global uncertainty and market attention.
global.whyItMatters: Any credible movement toward peace is worth noting for pastoral prayer and for framing hope without naivety.

canadian.title: B.C. mining company hires former U.S. homeland security secretary Kristi Noem - CTV News
canadian.summary: A B.C. mining company has hired former U.S. homeland security secretary Kristi Noem, adding a political dimension to a provincial business story.
canadian.whyItMatters: This touches B.C.’s public witness, cross-border influence, and the ethical questions Christians may ask about power, industry, and public leadership.

canadian.title: Kristi Noem hired by B.C. mining company - Global News
canadian.summary: Global News reports on the same developing B.C. mining story, underscoring its visibility in Canadian public conversation.
canadian.whyItMatters: When a provincial company makes a high-profile political hire, pastors may want awareness of the broader civic and moral discussion around it.

local.title: B.C. mining company hires former U.S. homeland security secretary Kristi Noem - CTV News
local.summary: A Vancouver-area news outlet reports that a B.C. mining company has brought on former U.S. homeland security secretary Kristi Noem.
local.whyItMatters: This is the clearest B.C.-relevant development in the results and may surface in local conversations about business, politics, and values.

persecutedChurch.title: China detains two leaders of influential underground church - BBC
persecutedChurch.summary: Chinese authorities detained two leaders of a major underground church after a Sunday raid in which more than 30 members and leaders were taken for questioning.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: This is a direct call to pray for imprisoned believers, courageous pastors, and churches facing state pressure for simply gathering to worship.

persecutedChurch.title: Chinese police swarm church, detain dozens in Sunday morning raid - Newsweek
persecutedChurch.summary: Newsweek reports the latest crackdown on an unregistered Chinese church, with authorities defending the action as part of state control over religion.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: It helps keep religious freedom visible before the congregation and can inform pastoral prayer for the global body of Christ.

leadership.title: When compliance kills trust: The psychological safety blind spot - HR Executive
leadership.summary: The piece argues that more rules and reporting channels do not automatically create trust; employees speak up when leaders show fairness, transparency, and follow-through.
leadership.whyItMatters: Helpful for church staff culture: people need to feel safe enough to tell the truth, raise concerns, and disagree without fear.

goodNews.title: Yanks take on a different kind of yard work in HOPE Week outing - MLB.com
goodNews.summary: During HOPE Week, Yankees players and staff served a neighbor in need by cleaning up a backyard and supporting a nonprofit that provides free lawn care for older adults, veterans, and struggling households.
goodNews.whyItMatters: It is a simple, concrete picture of loving your neighbor in action—useful encouragement for service-minded ministry.
